2605.18045.title: Confidence-Gated Robot Autonomy: When Does Uncertainty Actually Help?
2605.18045.paper_summary: Robotic systems often use predictive uncertainty to decide whether to act autonomously or defer to a fallback policy. In threshold-gated autonomy, uncertainty matters mainly through its ability to rank likely errors. Standard metrics such as expected calibration error and AUROC do not directly test whether uncertainty changes act/defer decisions. We therefore evaluate uncertainty using Spearman rank correlation, paired bootstrap equivalence testing, and act/defer agreement. Across three temporal activity-recognition benchmarks, we find a dataset-dependent competence regime below which uncertainty provides a weak and unstable error ranking. Above this regime, softmax heuristics, MC Dropout, and ensembles produce similar gating behavior, while threshold choice has a much larger effect on execution outcomes. A multi-seed embodied simulation shows the same pattern for collision rate and cost once realized autonomy is matched. Under temporal covariate shift, ranking quality remains stable, but fine grained semantic OOD detection remains near chance. These results suggest that simple uncertainty proxies can suffice for selective gating once the base model is competent, but not for semantic novelty detection.
2605.18045.paper_summary_zh: 機器人系統通常使用預測不確定性來決定是否自主行動或依賴備用政策。在閾值門控自主性中，不確定性主要通過其對可能錯誤的排名能力來影響結果。標準指標如預期校準誤差和AUROC並不直接測試不確定性是否改變行動/延遲決策。因此，我們使用斯皮爾曼排名相關性、配對自助等價性測試和行動/延遲一致性來評估不確定性。在三個時間活動識別基準中，我們發現一個依賴數據集的能力範疇，在此範疇下不確定性提供的錯誤排名較弱且不穩定。在此範疇之上，softmax啟發式、MC Dropout和集成方法產生類似的門控行為，而閾值選擇對執行結果的影響則大得多。一個多種種子體現模擬顯示，在實現的自主性匹配後，碰撞率和成本的模式相同。在時間協變移動下，排名質量保持穩定，但精細的語義OOD檢測仍接近隨機。這些結果表明，簡單的不確定性代理在基礎模型具備能力後可以用於選擇性門控，但對於語義新穎性檢測則不夠。
2605.18045.author: Johannes A. Gaus et.al.
2605.18045.authors: Johannes A. Gaus, Jhon P. F. Charaja, Daniel Haeufle
